---
title: "STAT 306 Project Code"
format: html
editor: visual
---

```{r}
library(ggplot2)
```

# Introduction

## Read Data

```{r}
insurance <- read.csv("insurance.csv")
summary(insurance)
```

# Analysis

## EDA

```{r}
# Plot: Age vs charges
ggplot(insurance, aes(x = age, y = charges)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  labs(title = "Age vs Charges",
       x = "Age",
       y = "Charges")

```

```{r}
# Plot: BMI vs charges
ggplot(insurance, aes(x = bmi, y = charges)) +
  geom_point(alpha=0.5) +
  labs(title = "BMI vs Charges",
       x = "BMI",
       y = "Charges")
```

```{r}
# Plot: Boxplot of smoker v.s. charges
ggplot(insurance, aes(x = smoker, y = charges)) +
  geom_boxplot() +
  labs(title = "Smoker vs Charges",
       x = "Smoker",
       y = "Charges")

```

```{r}
# Plot: Distribution of charges (histogram)
p1 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = charges)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 40, fill = "steelblue", color = "white") +
  labs(title = "Distribution of Insurance Charges",
       x = "Charges (USD)", y = "Count") +
  theme_minimal()

p1
ggsave("plot1_charges_distribution.png", plot = p1, width = 7, height = 5, dpi = 300)

p1

```

### Take log transform for charges

```{r}
insurance$log_charges <- log(insurance$charges)

p2 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = log_charges)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "#4472C4", color = "white", bins = 30) +
  labs(
    title = "Distribution of Log Insurance Charges",
    x = "Log Charges (log USD)",
    y = "Count"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggsave("log_charges_histogram.png", plot = p2, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
p2
```

## Ages VS log(charges) by smoking status

```{r}
p3 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = age, y = log_charges, color = smoker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("no" = "#4472C4", "yes" = "#E05C5C"),
                     name = "Smoker") +
  labs(
    title = "Age vs. Log Insurance Charges by Smoking Status",
    x = "Age (years)",
    y = "Log Charges (log USD)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p3

ggsave("plot3_age_vs_log_charges.png", plot = p3, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## BMI vs. Charges by Smoking Status

```{r}
p4 <- ggplot(insurance, aes(x = bmi, y = log_charges, color = smoker)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.5) +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("no" = "#4472C4", "yes" = "#E05C5C"),
                     name = "Smoker") +
  labs(
    title = "BMI vs. Log Insurance Charges by Smoking Status",
    x = "BMI (kg/m²)",
    y = "Log Charges (log USD)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p4

ggsave("plot4_bmi_vs_log_charges.png", plot = p4, width = 8, height = 5, dpi = 300)
```

## Model Fitting

```{r}
# Fit an addictive model
insurance$log_charges <- log(insurance$charges)
reg1=lm(log_charges~age+bmi+smoker,data=insurance)
summary(reg1)
```

```{r}
library(broom)
library(dplyr)
exp(reg1$coefficients)
tidy(reg1, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(across(c(estimate, conf.low, conf.high), exp)) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = term, y = estimate, ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high)) +
  geom_pointrange(color = "#4472C4", size = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 1, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +  # 注意基准线变成1
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Exponentiated Coefficients with 95% CI",
       x = "", y = "Multiplicative Effect on Charges") +
  theme_bw()
```
```{r}
# Residual and Q-Q normal Plot
library(patchwork)

p_resid <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(x = fitted, y = residuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "#4472C4") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted",
       x = "Fitted Values (log scale)",
       y = "Residuals") +
  theme_bw()

p_qq <- ggplot(diag_data, aes(sample = residuals)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.3, color = "#4472C4") +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Normal Q-Q Plot",
       x = "Theoretical Quantiles",
       y = "Sample Quantiles") +
  theme_bw()

combined <- p_resid + p_qq

ggsave("diagnostic_plots.png", plot = combined, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
combined
```

```{r}
# fit an interaction model
reg2=lm(log_charges~(age+bmi)*smoker,data=insurance)
summary(reg2)

```


```{r}
exp(reg2$coefficients)
tidy(reg2, conf.int = TRUE) %>%
  filter(term != "(Intercept)") %>%
  mutate(
    term = recode(term,
      "age"          = "Age",
      "bmi"          = "BMI (non-smoker)",
      "smokeryes"    = "Smoker",
      "bmi:smokeryes"= "BMI × Smoker"
    ),
    significant = p.value < 0.05
  ) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = reorder(term, estimate), y = estimate, 
             ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high,
             color = significant)) +
  geom_pointrange(size = 0.8) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "grey50") +
  scale_color_manual(values = c("TRUE" = "#4472C4", "FALSE" = "#aaaaaa"),
                     labels = c("TRUE" = "p < 0.05", "FALSE" = "p ≥ 0.05"),
                     name = "Significance") +
  coord_flip() +
  labs(title = "Coefficient Estimates with 95% CI (Interaction Model)",
       x = "", y = "Estimate (log scale)") +
  theme_bw()
```


```{r}
# Residual and Q-Q plot
diag_data2 <- data.frame(
  fitted    = fitted(reg2),
  residuals = residuals(reg2)
)

p_resid2 <- ggplot(diag_data2, aes(x = fitted, y = residuals)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.3, color = "#4472C4") +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = "red") +
  labs(title = "Residuals vs Fitted (Interaction Model)",
       x = "Fitted Values (log scale)",
       y = "Residuals") +
  theme_bw()

p_qq2 <- ggplot(diag_data2, aes(sample = residuals)) +
  stat_qq(alpha = 0.3, color = "#4472C4") +
  stat_qq_line(color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(title = "Normal Q-Q Plot (Interaction Model)",
       x = "Theoretical Quantiles",
       y = "Sample Quantiles") +
  theme_bw()

combined2 <- p_resid2 + p_qq2
ggsave("diagnostic_plots_interaction.png", plot = combined2, width = 12, height = 5, dpi = 300)
combined2
```
